title: Out-Of-Fold Hazard Encodings
summary: Discrete-time survival framing treats next-lap stop probability as conditional hazard.
rationale: Discrete-time survival framing treats next-lap stop probability as conditional hazard. Smoothed historical hazard by lap, race, stint, compound, and tyre-age bin can capture schedule-like pit windows that tree splits may not learn cleanly from sparse categories.
implementation_hint: Create OOF target encodings only within training folds for groups such as Race-LapNumber, RaceProgressBin-Compound, Stint-TyreLifeBin, Year-Race-Stint, Driver-Race. Use Bayesian smoothing toward global PitNextLap rate; apply learned maps to validation/test with global fallback.
expected_effect: Adds compact prior probabilities for pit windows and high-cardinality interactions, potentially lifting AUC if validation is leakage-safe.
risk: Naive target encoding will leak badly; all encodings must be fold-aware, and validation should group or time-split by race/year where possible.
